skill.name: seo-snippet-hunter
skill.description: Formats content to be eligible for featured snippets and SERP features. Creates snippet-optimized content blocks based on best practices. Use PROACTIVELY for question-based content.
skill.summary: Formats content to be eligible for featured snippets and SERP features. Creates snippet-optimized co...
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported external command and weak cryptographic algorithm patterns, but review found no executable code, shell invocation, cryptography, network access, or prompt injection. The flagged lines are Markdown formatting, a resource filename reference, and SEO prose, so the skill is safe to publish with low residual risk.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backticks Flagged as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged backticks at SKILL.md lines 25 and 81-90. Line 25 references a Markdown filename, and lines 81-90 are a fenced Markdown output example, not Ruby or shell execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file contains instructional Markdown only. The flagged backticks delimit text and an example block, with no code interpreter or command execution path.

low_findings.title: False Positive: SEO Text Flagged as Weak Cryptography
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged SKILL.md lines 3 and 51 as weak cryptographic algorithm indicators. These lines contain the words 'description' and 'descriptions' in SEO guidance, not cryptographic code or configuration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The matches are ordinary English words in a Markdown skill description. No cryptographic primitive, library call, or security-sensitive context appears in the reviewed file.

content.value_statement: Search pages often need concise answers, clear structure, and query-focused sections to qualify for SERP features. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code format content blocks for featured snippets, PAA answers, FAQs, and comparison tables.
actual_capabilities: Formats direct answer paragraphs for question-based search queries.
Creates list, table, and FAQ structures for snippet eligibility.
Suggests People Also Ask question and answer pairs.
Recommends content placement near the beginning of a page.
Identifies suitable schema types such as FAQPage and HowTo.
Provides multiple concise answer variations for the same query.
limitations: Does not guarantee featured snippet ranking or SERP placement.
Does not perform live keyword research or search result scraping.
Does not validate schema markup with external testing tools.
Requires source content, target queries, or competitor context from the user.

use_cases.title: Refresh Blog Answers
use_cases.description: Turn long article sections into direct answers, supporting bullets, and FAQ entries for search visibility.

use_cases.title: Structure Product Comparisons
use_cases.description: Create concise comparison tables and answer blocks for commercial search queries.

use_cases.title: Plan SERP-Focused Briefs
use_cases.description: Convert a target query list into snippet formats, placement notes, and schema recommendations.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Direct Answer
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to write a 40-60 word featured snippet answer for: [question]. Include a short supporting bullet list.

prompt_templates.title: Optimize an Existing Section
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to rewrite this section for featured snippet eligibility. Target query: [query]. Content: [paste section].

prompt_templates.title: Build a Snippet Package
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced content planning for several SERP formats.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to create paragraph, list, table, and FAQ snippet options for these target queries: [queries].

prompt_templates.title: Audit Competitor Snippet Gaps
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced comparison against known SERP examples.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to compare my content with these competitor snippet examples. Recommend answer formats, missing entities, and schema targets.

output_examples.input: A user asks for a featured snippet answer to 'What is technical SEO?'
output_examples.output: A concise definition appears first, followed by three supporting points about crawling, indexing, and page performance.

output_examples.input: A user provides product comparison content for two tools.
output_examples.output: The response recommends a comparison table, a short winner summary, and FAQ questions for commercial search intent.

output_examples.input: A user shares five People Also Ask questions.
output_examples.output: The response creates direct answer blocks, placement guidance, and schema suggestions for each question.

best_practices: Provide the target query, search intent, and existing content before requesting optimization.
Keep direct answers short, specific, and placed near the related heading.
Review recommendations against current SEO guidelines before publishing.
anti_patterns: Do not use the skill as proof that a page will win a featured snippet.
Do not ask it to invent search volume, rankings, or competitor data.
Do not publish generated schema without validation and editorial review.

faq.question: What does this skill optimize?
faq.answer: It optimizes content structure for featured snippets, People Also Ask answers, FAQ sections, lists, and comparison tables.

faq.question: Can it guarantee a featured snippet?
faq.answer: No. It improves formatting and clarity, but search engines decide ranking and SERP feature placement.

faq.question: Does it perform live SERP research?
faq.answer: No. Users must provide queries, existing content, or competitor examples for analysis.

faq.question: Can it create schema markup plans?
faq.answer: Yes. It can suggest schema types such as FAQPage and HowTo, but validation is still required.

faq.question: Who should use this skill?
faq.answer: SEO specialists, content marketers, editors, and site owners can use it to structure answer-focused content.

faq.question: Which tools support this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
